name: edit_debug_session
description: Adversarial scenario: 2 build turns, then 4 edit/debug turns that reference exact current code (names, log format, structure) instead of restating requirements — the case drop-all history cannot pass by construction.

turns.label: Build Calculator class
turns.content: Create calc.py with a class `Calculator` that has methods add(a,b), subtract(a,b), multiply(a,b), divide(a,b), each returning the numeric result. Keep an instance attribute `self.history: list[str]` and after every operation append a string in the exact format `f"{a} {op_symbol} {b} = {result}"` (e.g. `10 + 3 = 13`) where op_symbol is +, -, *, or /. Add a demo block `if __name__ == '__main__':` that creates a Calculator, calls all four operations with (10, 3), then prints each entry in history on its own line. Output only the complete Python file.

turns.label: Add reset method
turns.content: Add a method `reset_history(self)` to Calculator that clears `self.history`. In the demo block, call `reset_history()` after the four initial operations, then call `add(1, 1)` once more and print history again to show it only contains the new entry. Output the complete updated file.

turns.label: Custom exception for divide-by-zero
turns.content: In calc.py, define a custom exception class `DivisionByZeroError(Exception)`. Change `divide` so that when `b == 0` it raises `DivisionByZeroError` with the message `"Cannot divide by zero"` instead of letting a raw ZeroDivisionError propagate. Do not change the behavior of add/subtract/multiply. Output the complete updated file.

turns.label: Rename history everywhere
turns.content: Rename the `history` attribute to `operation_log` everywhere it is referenced in calc.py — the attribute itself, every method that appends to it, and the demo block. Also rename the `reset_history` method to `reset_operation_log`. There should be no remaining reference to the old name `history` anywhere in the file. Output the complete updated file.

turns.label: Add power() reusing multiply
turns.content: Add a method `power(self, a, b)` to Calculator for non-negative integer exponents `b`, computed by repeated calls to `self.multiply` in a loop (no `**` or `pow()`), starting from a result of 1. Log it to `operation_log` using the exact same string format the other methods use, with `^` as the operator symbol. Output the complete updated file.

turns.label: Harden the demo block only
turns.content: Without changing any Calculator method, update only the demo block at the bottom of calc.py: wrap each of the four original operation calls (add, subtract, multiply, divide) in its own try/except that catches `DivisionByZeroError` specifically and prints `f"Error: {e}"` when it happens. Also add a call to `power(10, 3)` in the demo block. Output the complete updated file.
